name: Half-Notch Fusion Cell
description: A Navarch reactor squeezed into as small of a space as possible, with a capacitor integrated directly into the energy loop. This serves as the power source for the Navarchs' drones, but would be able to supply energy for the fighters or interceptors of a lesser faction.

name: Nanoplasma Thruster (Tiny)
description: Despite their usage of large, heavy ships, Navarch engines are rather refined. Instead of providing as much thrust as possible, these engines are only about as powerful as plasma engines, but sharply reduce the effectiveness of enemy displacing attacks.

name: Nanoplasma Thruster (Small)
description: This small nanoplasma thruster is the standard for Navarch fighters, and serves a secondary role on many of their larger ships. In addition to a moderate amount of kick, these engines include billions of nanothrusters that counteract the displacing effects of incoming attacks.

name: Nanoplasma Thruster (Medium)
description: This nanoplasma thruster is in an odd spot, size-wise; too big for fighters, too small for ships. However, it serves a critical role as a benchmark for performance tests.

name: Nanoplasma Thruster (Large)
description: These large nanoplasma thrusters are capable of pushing some of the Navarchs' lighter warships at significant speeds, but lack the grunt needed to move their largest ships.

name: Nanoplasma Thruster (Huge)
description: As large as some of the largest engines humans have ever built, these nanoplasma thrusters are capable of significantly offsetting an opponent's attempts to push a ship away.

name: Nanoplasma Thruster (Giant)
description: These enormous thrusters are the primary propulsion method for most Navarch warships; larger than the interceptors of some species, this set of thrusters will get any ship where it needs to go.

name: Nanoplasma Steering (Tiny)
description: Sometimes even the Navarchs just have a little space left over to adjust their ships' agility; these tiny engines are inadequate even for their fighters, but serve moderately well as tweaks to a larger engine set.

name: Nanoplasma Steering (Small)
description: This is the standard steering system for Navarch fighters, and for good reason; it provides decent thrust for its size, while being compact and providing resistance against enemy displacement attacks.

name: Nanoplasma Steering (Medium)
description: Commonly used as a secondary steering system, this medium-sized nanoplasma thruster offers good all-around performance. Like its sister thruster, it is often used as a benchmarking device.

name: Nanoplasma Steering (Large)
description: A nanoplasma engine of this size is said to contain more micro-propulsion blocks than a standard human body has cells. As such, it's no surprise that it's powerful while providing utility in the form of protecting from unexpected jerks in spaceflight as well as engine interference.

name: Nanoplasma Steering (Huge)
description: This steering system is generally either adequate or too large for most ships produced by the Navarchs, providing any ship a noticeable increase in agility while reducing the likelihood that an enemy attack leaves a ship still in the sky.

name: Nanoplasma Steering (Giant)
description: The largest nanoplasma steering set is reserved only for the largest of ships assembled by the Navarchs. Navarchs commonly joke that engines like these would be terrifying weapons, if only they had a practical amount of range.

name: Pantheon-Class Breach Sealant
description: When the Navarchs needed a shield generator for their largest ships, they sought to make a shield generator that would be able to level the playing field with any alien armada. This shield generator is so powerful that the shield often leaks into the hull, effectively replacing lost surface hull until a more permanent fix can be implemented.

name: Temple-Class Breach Sealant
description: The Temple-Class Breach Sealant is a mid-sized breach sealing system, intended either for middleweight warships or as a support to a larger Pantheon-class on a larger ship.

name: Throne-Class Breach Sealant
description: Utilizing techniques discovered during the creation of the larger Pantheon-Class Breach Sealant, the Throne-Class is a far smaller rig designed for small torpedo boats or fighters. A little over one-tenth the size of the Pantheon-Class, the Throne-Class serves its role well.

name: "Kecid" Rearmorer
description: This larger rearmorer is capable of repairing even the largest Navarch ships in a fraction of the time that a breach sealant unit of comparable size would, but requires an enormous amount of energy to keep running and has no ability to recover a ship's shields.

name: "Lythang" Rearmorer
description: Experimental Navarch vessels rely primarily on their cutting-edge armor rather than shield matricies. These odd "hull reactors" contain loops that allow them to circulate a gel that solidifies and crystallizes into this new armor when exposed to open space, allowing a ship to repair itself at great speed. The system takes a constant supply of energy to fabricate and circulate replacement fluid, but as a consequence, requires very little active energy when the hull is damaged.

name: "Prathon" Rearmorer
description: Experimental Navarch vessels rely primarily on their cutting-edge armor rather than shield matricies. These odd "hull reactors" contain loops that allow them to circulate a gel that solidifies and crystallizes into this new armor when exposed to open space, allowing a ship to repair itself at great speed. The system takes a constant supply of energy to fabricate and circulate replacement fluid, but as a consequence, requires very little active energy when the hull is damaged.

name: 20x Fusion Reactor
description: Though the design was not ever intended for reactors larger than the 10x, the Navarchs' super battleship design required energy beyond the realm of the 10x. This colossal rig's power output is enough to run a moderately sized spaceport.

name: 14x Fusion Reactor
description: Though the design was not ever intended for reactors larger than the 10x, the Navarchs' super battleship design required energy beyond the realm of the 10x. This colossal rig's power output is enough to run a moderately sized spaceport.

name: 10x Fusion Reactor
description: Once the largest reactor the Navarchs produced, the shift to overall larger vessels (over the mid-sized vessels once favoured by Navarch doctrine) has resulted in this reactor being just a medium-sized permutation of the typical Navarch fusion technology.

name: 6x Fusion Reactor
description: This smaller reactor serves as a secondary power source for the Navarchs' larger vessels.

name: 4x Fusion Reactor
description: This small fusion reactor serves as a midstep between the Navarchs' fighter-scale reactors and their much larger capital ship reactors, often not large enough to power a ship by itself, but small enough it can serve as a secondary reactor.

name: 2x Fusion Reactor
description: For their fighters, the Navarchs simply scaled down their pre-existing reactor design. This isn't a particularly elegant solution, but provides the necessary energy to run all of a Subvarietas' systems and weapons.

name: 1x Fusion Reactor
description: This is a miniscule generator by Navarch standards, allowing their smallest ships to boast significant electrical output while remaining compact.

name: 1/2x Reactor Cell
description: A Navarch Fusion reactor crammed into as small of a package as possible. The resultant system runs fairly warm, even for its size, but produces all the energy needed to run a Subforma drone and all of its faculties.

name: Cluster Battery
description: Named after collections of galaxies in space, this huge battery is capable of storing an alarming amount of energy.

name: Galaxy Battery
description: Whether used as a support to a larger battery pack or on its own, this battery grants its host ship a significant boost to energy capacity.

name: Planet Battery
description: This battery is only suitable for medium or light warships employed by the Navarchs, but for most other species, its size and capacity would better fit large capital warships.

name: Moon Battery
description: This relatively compact battery is used by small Navarch ships with low energy needs, such as their light carriers.

name: Accretion Capacitor
description: This larger capacitor was designed by the Navarchs for small ships with inconsistent energy needs, such as those fielding lots of anti-missile.

name: Comet Capacitor
description: This is a moderately sized capacitor, designed by the Navarchs for their fighters.

name: "Kepilora" Nominal Storage
description: Reserved for particularly large experimental vessels, this Nominal Storage system is capable of providing a good source of shields to any ship it's installed on; it does, however, run quite hot.

name: "Lycir" Nominal Storage
description: Though the Navarchs understand them well-enough to build them regularly, the exact workings of the Nominal Storage are a mystery to the human mind. The best explanation you've gotten is that the devices use a yottrite filament to trigger assorted actions in the battery during charging and discharging that produces shields and heat as a byproduct.

name: "Igirid" Nominal Storage
description: Nominal Storages are a curious set of devices cooked up by the Navarchs' experimental department; like normal batteries, they store energy, but the storage device itself produces a field as it charges and discharges, allow a ship to recover its shields passively without consuming any extra energy. 	Compared to typical Navarch batteries, Nominal Storages do not store quite as much energy per space, and generate some heat.

name: AHDU (Huge)
description: Particularly large Navarch battleships often find themselves installing many heat dissipation units. To attempt to simplify ship blueprints, the Navarchs designed this most impressive cooling system.

name: AHDU (Large)
description: This large implementation of the Advanced Heat Dissipation Unit is a powerful cooling system designed to offset the significant heat produced by their battleships.

name: AHDU (Medium)
description: This large implementation of the Advanced Heat Dissipation Unit is a powerful cooling system designed to offset the significant heat produced by their battleships.

name: AHDU (Small)
description: This small Heat Dissipation Unit is designed as a small add-on for most Navarch Vessels, but has use as primary cooling on some of their smallest ships.

name: Fuel Distillery
description: Because their ships are designed to operate both in- and out-of-atmosphere, the Navarchs have a particular distaste towards solar-reliant systems, and ramscoops are no exception. This large device distills fuel over time, allowing a ship to refuel even when solar energy is nonpresent. 	Due to the fact that its size and cooling requirements are on par with a midsized nuclear reactor, it is found only on support ships.

name: Spectral Jammer
description: This jammer broadcasts broad-wave light across radar and visible wavelengths, making it far more difficult for missiles to maintain a lock.

name: Analysis Scope
description: The Navarchs engage in combat at distances far greater than most species, so their scanners are tuned to work at extreme ranges. Despite only being twice the size of a human tactical scanner, this advanced scope is nearly 8 times as powerful.

name: Mass Mineral Proc Array
description: A typical Navarch mining fleet includes one central carrier responsible co-ordinating the entire fleet, and part of those duties include procing all asteroids in the vacinity for valuable minerals. This powerful scanner consumes some energy, but provides an unreal level of asteroid scanning.

name: Navarch Boarding Supremacy
description: With the claws in their arms acting as electrified harpoons, the Navarchs rarely have a need for artificial weapons; not to mention, they are unlikely to refuse to sell their technology to others, for the right price. However, should someone attempt to capture a Navarch ship by force, this labyrinthine network of choke-points, hidden flanks, automated defenses, and heavy gear for on-board personnel awaits them.

name: Quarg Loatherift
description: This unassuming outfit acts as a locus for powerful teleporters aboard Quarg ringworlds, allowing Quarg ships to warp in massive amounts of ultra-powerful missiles without worrying about running out of ammunition. The teleporter, however, does require significant energy and cooling to operate safely.

name: 14-103 Twin Battery
description: For their super battleships, the Navarchs designed this huge-bore turret. Though the reload time leaves much to be desired, the huge shots deliver crushing damage over a broad area; each shell could level a medium-sized human warship.

name: 10-77 Twin Battery
description: This is the primary weapon for the Navarchs' large battleships: a twin-barreled, wide-bore cannon that's slow to aim and fire but deals impeccable damage once it connects. The significant firing forces produced by the weapon require a special armored hardpoint (a barbette) to be installed.

name: 7-62 Twin Battery
description: This weapon is a far smaller caliber than its bigger brother, but has a longer relative barrel length to compensate, allowing its projectiles to fly similar distances despite their lighter payload. Despite the major downsizing, this turret still produces enough force when firing to require a barbette.

name: 4-26 Dual Turret
description: The 4-26 is designed as a secondary turret for the Navarchs' large battleships, or a more primary role for their smaller ships. One turret is not particularly impressive by itself, but when equipped en masse, they can be effective at wearing down opponents at a fair distance.

name: 1-18 Anti-Fighter Rig
description: For anti-fighter roles, this tiny, snappy turret is the Navarchs' first choice. Small enough to be mounted on nearly anything and capable of a freakishly high rate of fire, this turret is designed for close-ranged anti-swarm combat, with a wild spray and considerable damage output.

name: 1-24 Anti-Fighter Array
description: Developed for the Regio-class carrier, this larger implementation of the 1-18 model autocannon is designed to saturate an area in anti-fighter fire. Improvements to the design also include greatly improved velocity, and subsequently, increased range.

name: THEL Node
description: The Navarchs have relatively little experience with beam technology compared to their tried and true "energy shell" designs. This prototypical weapon (dubbed the Tactical High Energy Laser) is their first such combat-practical design; a huge beam that carves through all but the thickest armor in its path, though compared to its projectile-based bretheren it suffers in the shield damage department. 	While the weapon doesn't produce nearly as much firing force as its projectile-based counterparts, the energy and cooling requirements means it still needs a barbette to be handled properly.

name: Shock Blaster Delta
description: Shock Blasters were designed as a method of building turrets that could provide effective damage at range without needing a barbette. The Shock Blaster Delta is the largest design that was successful in its goal: A rapid-fire, anti-area blaster that drains the electrical energy of struck ships, able to be mounted on any ship with the capacities to support it and capable of long-distance operations.

name: 5-36 Bombardier
description: Found aboard the newly developed Subformae, these modest cannons are capable of delivering significant anti-capital damage, with the sophisticated projectile shapes causing a small amount of damage to hull regardless of the target's shield state.

name: 3-22 Bombardier
description: A highly specialized cannon designed for fighter-to-capital warfare. Though slightly smaller caliber than its battleship-mounted cousins, the 3-22 Bombardier is outfitted with armor-piercing projectile forms that cause immense damage on impact at the cost of poor performance against crowds. 	To counteract the considerable hit force buildup that can accrue on a target under fire from a large swarm of Subvarietas, the Navarchs have designed this weapon to pull a target slightly closer to the ship, significantly reducing the likelihood a sortie of Subvarietas will be kited away from their support fleet.

name: 0.6-16 Autocannon
description: An autocannon designed for use aboard the Subvarietas fighter. Though its per-shot damage is notably unimpressive, it is capable of a blistering rate of fire with shells that have a deceptively wide danger area, making it excellent for interfighter combat.

name: "Povenant" Anti-Scuttle
description: Anti-Scuttles are area-of-effect devices that produce a powerful shield recovery effect during combat. Though even this small model of Anti-Scuttle requires a substantial amount of space and energy generation to operate, it is capable of recovering the shields of surrounding ships rather readily. Though the physical "ring" of influence highlights where the Anti-Scuttle is most effective, the rig's radius of effect caps out at about double what is observable to the naked eye. Because they operate on similar principles to Breach Sealant, Anti-Scuttles are also capable of repairing some of a ship's hull (though at a greatly diminished rate, and only within the visible ring).

name: Monolithic Anti-Missile
description: This Anti-Missile system is specially designed for carriers or other ships with few hardpoints, but otherwise large capacity for weapons. Though the Formidable Anti-Missile offers more balanced performance, the Monolithic model has supreme range, allowing it to cast a wide net of defense over an allied fleet.

name: Formidable Anti-Missile
description: The Formidable Anti-Missile is the standard-issue missile defense system for Navarch ships. Though it doesn't quite pack as much punch-per-ton as its smaller brother, it has greater range and higher per-shot power, massively increasing the likelihood a single shot will be needed to thwart a missile.

name: Intimidating Anti-Missile
description: Designed for light ships with a large amount of hardpoints, the Intimidating Anti-Missile is the smallest anti-missile system offered by the Navarchs. Its range is quite unimpressive compared to their larger anti-missile systems, but allows a ship to keep itself clear of enemy missile barrages.

name: Phase Torpedo
description: Named for their ability to move through space, air, or water, these heavy torpedoes consume massive amounts of energy and move slowly, but inflict devastating damage on hit, to the point where no shield can prevent at least some hull damage from leaking through.

name: Lathe Mine Bay
description: Lathe Mines are a particularly brutal type of obstacle that the Navarchs designed to be laid out in great numbers, dealing crushing damage to any ship who dares to stumble into them. Using similar technology to the phase torpedo, Lathe Mines cause heavy damage even through shields. The unique design of the Lathe mines means that, though they're intended as a trap, they are capable of serving as a form of anti-area projectile in the right conditions.

name: Ransacker ALS
description: Ransacker Missiles are a surprisingly unrefined weapon; easily replaceable missiles that split into a wide barrage of smaller explosives, pelting enemy fleets with an enormous amount of cluster munitions. 	Ransackers are launched at a reduced launch pressure, reducing load on the ship and allowing 9 to be launched from a relatively compact platform. However, the fact that the projectiles are assembled on the spot means reload time leaves something to be desired.

name: Hyperdrive Malformer
description: True to its name, this weapon works in a similar method to a commonplace hyperdrive; it uses a large quantity of deuterium to produce a large explosion. However, instead of tucking it into a safe region of spacetime to accelerate the ship, the device warps the explosion a good distance in front of the firing ship, causing a devastating explosion. According to Navarch legend, this was the weapon that caused the Drak and Quarg to seal the Navarchs away. 	Somehow, the Navarchs have found a way to program the explosion in a way that it doesn't harm allied ships, meaning that, for a weapon of mass destruction, it's surprisingly safe to use.

name: Unbound Gauss Slug
description: This three-ton hunk of yottrite alloy is ammunition for the Unbound Gauss Drive. The acceleration the slugs experience is intense enough to distend the slug over the course of firing, but the result is an unstoppable column of destruction that cuts through all in its path.

name: Unbound Gauss Drive
description: The earliest phases of this weapon's development harken back to before the Navarchs even first came into contact with the Quarg; an early attempt at using energy to replace the fuel in a Hyperdrive's operation, allowing a planet to theoretically launch fighters without any need for deuterium or worry of fallout from the fusion explosion that powers a Hyperdrive. The device failed, and was shelved for millenia. 	Relatively recently, however, some attention was brought back to the device, with the hopes that it could be turned into an effective weapon against swarms of Quarg Automata. The resultant weapon fires enormous yottrite-alloy slugs at near-light speed, causing an explosive effect on target and effortlessly cutting through all in its way. 	(Note: The amount of contact per shot can vary with enemy size, so damage per second is approximate.)
